So, the mutant changed how offsets are handled, especially for non-positive values (zero or negative). This caused several existing tests related to DateTimeZone to start failing because their expected behavior was based on the original condition where only zero offset returns UTC. Now, any negative offset also does so, which might not be intended.

The key point is that this mutation introduced a change in how offsets are processed, leading to new failures and unexpected behaviors in other parts of the code. Therefore, this suggests that the mutant is indeed adjacent to a fault because it broke existing functionality and caused more tests to fail than expected.
